svg.title: 30-Day Accessibility Action Plan
svg.desc: A four-week plan for the month after finishing this course. Week one: pick the single most important fix — usually remediate your next-semester syllabus end-to-end. Week two and three: broader remediation — apply the same techniques to weekly slide decks and handouts, limit scope to one course. Week four: share one thing with a colleague and schedule a six-month follow-up review in your calendar.
svg.text: Your 30-Day Action Plan
What you'll actually do in the four weeks after this course
WEEK 1
The Kickoff Fix
One job — pick the single most used document.
• Remediate next-semester syllabus end-to-end.
• Run Microsoft Accessibility Checker. Target: 0 errors.
• Save as "CourseName-Syllabus-Accessible.docx".
• Tab through it to confirm reading order.
Why Week 1 only: a realistic, finishable win builds momentum.
WEEKS 2–3
Broader Remediation
Expand to the materials students use most.
• Pick ONE course. Audit every slide deck + handout.
• Fix built-in layouts, alt text, reading order, contrast.
• Verify captions on every assigned video.
• Replace any drag-and-drop quiz questions.
Why one course only: scope discipline beats sprawl every time.
WEEK 4
Share + Schedule the Follow-up
Make it culture, not just your calendar.
• Share ONE fix, tool, or discovery with a department colleague — casual hallway or Slack mention is enough.
• Add a calendar reminder titled "Accessibility Review" at the 6-month mark — use your Semester-Start Checklist.
• Reflect: what took longer than expected? What felt easier than expected? Write two sentences. Keep it.
Why share: culture moves one colleague at a time, not one memo at a time.
One finishable plan beats a perfect one you never start.
